mission.title: flux: HelmRelease Values from targetPath array
mission.description: HelmRelease Values from targetPath array. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current flux deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flux version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
helm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flux installation.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
### Describe the bug

Hello, I cannot seem to be able to use the `targetFrom` field to access an existing array within the `valuesFrom` object in a helm release.

steps.title: Apply the fix for HelmRelease Values from targetPath array
steps.description:
`spec.values` is documented to merge after `valuesFrom`
(maybe we made the wront behavior/API choice here seeing as there are two issues here with a misconception of this + Secrets/Configs typically have fewer, more specific, sensitive fields + the --set/valuesFrom style supports nested keys and
```yaml
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2beta1
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: release-name
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  values:
    confd:
      file.yaml:
        init_config:
          service: kafka-consumers
        instances:
          - kafka_connect_str: xxx:9092
            monitor_unlisted_consumer_groups: true
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "HelmRelease Values from targetPath array" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
`spec.values` is documented to merge after `valuesFrom`
(maybe we made the wront behavior/API choice here seeing as there are two issues here with a misconception of this + Secrets/Configs typically have fewer, more specific, sensitive fields + the --set/valuesFrom style supports nested keys and array indexing)

In your example, your `instances` list is first being built by valuesFrom, and then
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2beta1
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: release-name
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  values:
    confd:
      file.yaml:
        init_config:
          service: kafka-consumers
        instances:
          - kafka_connect_str: xxx:9092
            monitor_unlisted_consumer_groups: true

valuesFrom:
    - kind: Secret
      name: secrets
      valuesKey: username
      targetPath: confd.file\.yaml.instances[0].sasl_plain_username
    - kind: Secret
      name: secrets
      valuesKey: password
      targetPath: confd.file\.yaml.instances[0].sasl_plain_password
